name: EverMemOS
content: A self-organizing memory operating system for LLMs that implements an engram-inspired lifecycle to transform fragmented episodic experiences into coherent, stable knowledge structures.

name: Episodic Trace Formation
content: Phase I of EverMemOS. Converts interaction history streams into atomic memory units called MemCells through a pipeline of Contextual Segmentation, Narrative Synthesis, and Structural Derivation.

name: Semantic Consolidation
content: Phase II of EverMemOS. Organizes MemCells into thematic, higher-level structures called MemScenes and updates a compact User Profile from aggregated evidence.

name: Reconstructive Recollection
content: Phase III of EverMemOS. Performs agentic, MemScene-guided retrieval modeled as an active reconstruction process, involving MemScene selection, filtering, and verification.

name: MemCell
content: The atomic memory unit in EverMemOS, defined as a tuple c = (E, F, P, M) representing Episode, Atomic Facts, Foresight, and Metadata.

name: MemScene
content: A thematic, higher-level semantic structure in EverMemOS formed by clustering related MemCells during the Semantic Consolidation phase.

name: User Profile
content: A compact representation in EverMemOS containing explicit facts and implicit user traits, updated online from scene summaries during Semantic Consolidation.

name: LoCoMo
content: A benchmark for evaluating memory systems, containing 1,540 questions over 10 ultra-long dialogues (~9K tokens each) spanning single-hop, multi-hop, and temporal questions.

name: LongMemEval
content: A benchmark for evaluating memory systems, with an S-setting of ~115k tokens per conversation and 500 questions requiring full-history parsing across core capabilities.

name: PersonaMem-v2
content: A dataset used for ablation studies, evaluating the effect of a consolidated user profile alongside episodic retrieval.

name: Memory Operating Systems
content: A category of memory systems for LLMs, including Nemori, MemoryOS, Mem0, Zep, and MemOS, which focus on storage optimization or fact maintenance.

name: Context Window Extension
content: A memory mechanism for LLMs involving techniques like sparse attention and recurrence to expand context, limited by the 'Lost-in-the-Middle' phenomenon.

name: Retrieval-Augmented and Parametric Memory
content: Memory mechanisms for LLMs including RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) and parametric approaches, which face issues with retrieval reliability and forgetting.

name: implements
content: EverMemOS implements this as its first phase to create MemCells from raw interaction streams.

name: implements
content: EverMemOS implements this as its second phase to organize MemCells into MemScenes and update the User Profile.

name: implements
content: EverMemOS implements this as its third phase for agentic, scene-guided memory retrieval.

name: produces
content: The Episodic Trace Formation phase transforms interaction history into atomic MemCells.

name: organizes into
content: The Semantic Consolidation phase clusters related MemCells to form higher-level MemScenes.

name: updates
content: The Semantic Consolidation phase updates the compact User Profile from aggregated scene evidence.

name: guides
content: MemScenes guide the agentic retrieval process in the Reconstructive Recollection phase.

name: evaluated on
content: EverMemOS was experimentally evaluated on the LoCoMo benchmark, showing significant performance improvements.

name: evaluated on
content: EverMemOS was experimentally evaluated on the LongMemEval benchmark, showing significant performance improvements.

name: used in ablation on
content: An ablation study on PersonaMem-v2 evaluated the complementary benefit of the User Profile.

name: outperforms
content: Experiments show EverMemOS significantly outperforms state-of-the-art memory operating systems like Zep, Mem0, and MemOS.

name: addresses limitation of
content: EverMemOS is proposed to address limitations like 'Lost-in-the-Middle' and high cost associated with simply extending the context window.

name: addresses limitation of
content: EverMemOS is proposed to address limitations like poor integration and lack of consolidation in flat, retrieval-based memory approaches.

name: MAGMA
content: Multi-Graph based Agentic Memory Architecture for AI Agents. A system designed to overcome limitations of existing Memory-Augmented Generation (MAG) approaches by representing memory across four orthogonal graphs and using policy-guided traversal for retrieval.

name: Memory-Augmented Generation (MAG)
content: A solution to LLM limitations, using an external memory to record interaction histories. Contrasts with static RAG by maintaining a time-variant memory that evolves via a feedback loop.

name: Large Language Models (LLMs)
content: Models with limitations in handling long-term context due to finite attention windows, attention dilution, positional encoding issues, and the 'lost-in-the-middle' phenomenon.

name: LoCoMo
content: A benchmark by Maharana et al., 2024 for assessing long-range temporal and causal retrieval in ultra-long conversations (average 9K tokens).

name: LongMemEval
content: A benchmark by Wu et al., 2024 as a large-scale stress-test for evaluating scalability and memory retention stability over extended interactions (average context length exceeding 100K tokens).

name: Data Structure Layer
content: A logical layer in MAGMA's architecture providing unified storage with a Vector Database and four Relation Graphs: Semantic, Temporal, Causal, and Entity. Formalized as a time-variant directed multigraph G_t = (N_t, E_t).

name: Semantic Graph
content: One of the four relation graphs in MAGMA (E_sem). Contains undirected edges based on cosine similarity between event node embeddings.

name: Temporal Graph
content: One of the four relation graphs in MAGMA (E_temp). Contains strictly ordered pairs of event nodes based on timestamps.

name: Causal Graph
content: One of the four relation graphs in MAGMA (E_causal). Contains directed edges representing logical entailment between events, inferred during asynchronous consolidation.

name: Entity Graph
content: One of the four relation graphs in MAGMA (E_ent). Contains edges connecting event nodes to entity nodes.

name: Query Process
content: A logical layer in MAGMA's architecture for inference. Contains an Intent-Aware Router, Adaptive Topological Retrieval, and a Context Synthesizer. Performs policy-guided graph traversal for retrieval.

name: Adaptive Traversal Policy
content: A core component of MAGMA's Query Process. Guides context expansion from anchor nodes using Heuristic Beam Search, calculating a dynamic transition score that fuses structural alignment and semantic affinity.

name: Memory Evolution (Write/Update)
content: A dual-stream process in MAGMA for transitioning the memory graph from G_t to G_{t+1}. Decouples latency-sensitive ingestion from compute-intensive consolidation.

name: Fast Path (Synaptic Ingestion)
content: Part of MAGMA's Memory Evolution. Handles non-blocking operations on the critical interaction path: event segmentation, vector indexing, updating the temporal backbone, and queueing for the slow path.

name: Slow Path (Structural Consolidation)
content: Part of MAGMA's Memory Evolution. An asynchronous background worker that dequeues events and uses an LLM to infer latent causal and entity connections, densifying the graph structure.

name: A-MEM
content: A prior MAG scheme and baseline system for comparison. MAGMA outperforms it on benchmarks.

name: Nemori
content: A baseline MAG system for comparison. MAGMA outperforms it on benchmarks.

name: MemoryOS
content: A baseline agentic memory system for comparison. MAGMA outperforms it on benchmarks.

name: Full Context
content: A baseline where the entire conversation history is fed into the LLM's context window. MAGMA outperforms it on benchmarks.

name: Event Extraction Prompt
content: A prompt type in MAGMA's prompt library. Employs a strict JSON schema to capture entities, relationships, and temporal markers from events to ensure robustness.

name: Query-Adaptive QA Prompt
content: A prompt type in MAGMA's prompt library. Begins with a strict persona definition and appends dynamic reasoning instructions based on the Router's intent classification.

name: Evaluation Prompt (LLM-as-a-Judge)
content: A prompt type in MAGMA's prompt library. Used for semantic scoring, evaluating alignment between a generated response and ground truth on a continuous scale.

name: designed to overcome
content: MAGMA is designed to overcome the structural limitations of existing MAG approaches, such as monolithic memory stores and reliance on semantic similarity.

name: addresses limitations of
content: MAG is introduced as a solution to the limitations of LLMs in handling long-term context and long-horizon reasoning.

name: evaluated on
content: Comprehensive experiments evaluate MAGMA's reasoning effectiveness on the LoCoMo benchmark.

name: evaluated on
content: Comprehensive experiments evaluate MAGMA's scalability and memory retention on the LongMemEval benchmark.

name: contains
content: The Data Structure Layer stores memory items across four orthogonal graphs, including the Semantic Graph.

name: contains
content: The Data Structure Layer stores memory items across four orthogonal graphs, including the Temporal Graph.

name: contains
content: The Data Structure Layer stores memory items across four orthogonal graphs, including the Causal Graph.

name: contains
content: The Data Structure Layer stores memory items across four orthogonal graphs, including the Entity Graph.

name: performs
content: The Query Process orchestrates retrieval via a policy-guided graph traversal, which is executed by the Adaptive Traversal Policy.

name: traverses
content: The Adaptive Traversal Policy performs heuristic beam search to navigate and retrieve information from the multi-graph data structure.

name: comprises
content: The Memory Evolution process is a dual-stream pipeline, with the Fast Path handling immediate, latency-sensitive ingestion.

name: comprises
content: The Memory Evolution process is a dual-stream pipeline, with the Slow Path handling asynchronous, compute-intensive consolidation.

name: outperforms
content: Experimental results on benchmarks show MAGMA achieves higher accuracy and lower latency than the A-MEM baseline.

name: outperforms
content: Experimental results on benchmarks show MAGMA achieves higher accuracy and lower latency than the Nemori baseline.

name: outperforms
content: Experimental results on benchmarks show MAGMA achieves higher accuracy than the MemoryOS baseline.

name: outperforms
content: Experimental results on benchmarks show MAGMA achieves higher accuracy with drastically reduced token usage compared to the Full Context baseline.

name: uses
content: The Slow Path uses an LLM prompted by the Event Extraction Prompt to infer latent causal and entity connections from dequeued events.

name: uses
content: The Query Process uses the Query-Adaptive QA Prompt to generate responses, injecting dynamic instructions based on the Router's intent classification.

name: uses
content: The experimental evaluation uses the LLM-as-a-Judge prompt for semantic scoring to compare MAGMA's outputs against ground truth.

name: CompassMem
content: An event-centric memory framework for LLM-based agents that organizes memory as a structured hierarchical Event Graph to actively guide searching and reasoning.

name: Event Graph
content: A structured hierarchical memory representation where experiences are stored as coherent event units connected by explicit logical relations.

name: Event Segmentation Theory (EST)
content: A cognitive science framework explaining how humans segment continuous perceptual experience into meaningful event units, which inspires CompassMem's memory segmentation.

name: Incremental Hierarchical Memory Construction
content: The process of building the Event Graph, involving event segmentation, relation extraction, and incremental graph updates via Node Fusion & Expansion and Topic Evolution.

name: Event Segmentation
content: Process where an LLM segments an input stream into coherent events, each defined by observation span, temporal info, semantic summary, and participants.

name: Relation Extraction
content: Process where an LLM extracts logical relations (e.g., causal, temporal) between events from an open-ended predicate set.

name: Topic Evolution
content: A layer over accumulated events where topics represent semantic clusters, initialized via K-means and updated with new events or periodic re-clustering.

name: Active Multi-Path Memory Search
content: The inference process where agents actively navigate the Event Graph using a Planner, multiple Explorers, and a Responder to gather evidence for complex queries.

name: Planner
content: An LLM-based agent that decomposes a query into subgoals and performs gap-aware refinement to generate refined queries if search fails.

name: Explorer
content: Multiple LLM-based agents that perform localization and navigate the Event Graph by choosing actions (SKIP, EXPAND, ANSWER) at each node based on query and context.

name: Responder
content: An LLM-based agent invoked when search is complete to generate the final output from the distilled evidence set.

name: LoCoMo
content: A benchmark for conversational question answering used to evaluate memory frameworks like CompassMem.

name: NarrativeQA
content: A benchmark for narrative understanding used to evaluate memory frameworks like CompassMem.

name: Mem0
content: A baseline memory framework for agents used for comparison in experiments.

name: MemoryOS
content: A baseline memory framework for agents used for comparison in experiments.

name: A-Mem
content: A baseline memory framework using structured approaches, used for comparison in experiments.

name: CAM
content: A baseline memory framework for agents used for comparison in experiments.

name: HippoRAG
content: A baseline memory framework for agents used for comparison in experiments.

name: GPT-4o-mini
content: A backbone language model used in experiments for CompassMem and baselines.

name: Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct
content: A backbone language model used in experiments for CompassMem and baselines.

name: BGE-M3
content: An embedding model used for semantic similarity in the CompassMem framework.

name: is inspired by
content: The design of CompassMem's event-centric memory framework is inspired by the cognitive science theory of Event Segmentation.

name: organizes memory as
content: The core idea of CompassMem is to organize agent memory as a structured hierarchical Event Graph.

name: performs
content: CompassMem constructs its memory through an incremental process of event segmentation and graph building.

name: enables
content: During inference, CompassMem enables an active, logic-aware search process over the constructed Event Graph.

name: consists of
content: The memory construction process involves segmenting the input stream into coherent events.

name: consists of
content: The memory construction process involves extracting logical relations between the segmented events.

name: includes
content: The memory construction process includes maintaining a topic layer over events for semantic clustering.

name: uses
content: The search process uses a Planner agent to decompose queries and refine goals.

name: uses
content: The search process uses multiple Explorer agents to navigate the graph in parallel.

name: uses
content: The search process uses a Responder agent to synthesize the final answer from collected evidence.

name: is evaluated on
content: The performance of CompassMem is tested on the LoCoMo conversational QA benchmark.

name: is evaluated on
content: The performance of CompassMem is tested on the NarrativeQA narrative understanding benchmark.

name: is compared to
content: Experimental results compare CompassMem's performance against the Mem0 baseline.

name: is compared to
content: Experimental results compare CompassMem's performance against the MemoryOS baseline.

name: is compared to
content: Experimental results compare CompassMem's performance against the A-Mem baseline.

name: is compared to
content: Experimental results compare CompassMem's performance against the CAM baseline.

name: is compared to
content: Experimental results compare CompassMem's performance against the HippoRAG baseline.

name: uses model
content: Experiments for CompassMem are run using GPT-4o-mini as a backbone language model.

name: uses model
content: Experiments for CompassMem are run using Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct as a backbone language model.

name: uses embeddings from
content: CompassMem uses the BGE-M3 model for generating embeddings to compute semantic similarity.

name: Agent Memory System
content: An evolving memory state M_t defined by three conceptual operators: Memory Formation (F), Memory Evolution (E), and Memory Retrieval (R). It is the internal substrate for learning, adaptation, and autonomy in LLM-driven agents.

name: Memory Formation (F)
content: A conceptual operator that selectively transforms informational artifacts (e.g., tool outputs, reasoning traces) into memory candidates.

name: Memory Evolution (E)
content: A conceptual operator that integrates memory candidates by consolidating redundant entries, resolving conflicts, discarding low-utility information, or restructuring.

name: Memory Retrieval (R)
content: A conceptual operator that constructs a task-aware query to retrieve a formatted memory signal m_i^t for the LLM policy.

name: LLM Memory
content: Mechanisms that directly modify the model's internal state (e.g., architectural changes for long context, KV cache management) to expand representational capacity. Lacks cross-task persistence and deliberate memory operations.

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
content: A family of techniques for retrieving external information to augment LLM generation. Includes Modular RAG (static pipeline), Graph RAG (graph-structured knowledge), and Agentic RAG (autonomous retrieval decisions).

name: Context Engineering
content: A resource management methodology optimizing the information payload within the LLM's context window. Focuses on structural organization, tool integration, and communication protocols.

name: Token-level Memory
content: A memory form that is symbolic, addressable, and transparent. Suitable for multi-turn chatbots, personalized agents, and high-stake domains requiring explicit reasoning and accountability.

name: Flat Memory (1D)
content: A topology-free memory form (e.g., vector store) offering simplicity and scalability for broad recall and episodic accumulation, but lacks explicit relational organization.

name: Planar Memory (2D)
content: A graph-based memory form that establishes links between nodes to leverage collective synergies and support relational traversal, but can lack hierarchy and be costly to construct.

name: Hierarchical Memory (3D)
content: A memory form organizing information across layers with inter-level connections. Includes 'Pyramid' (coarse-to-fine querying) and 'Multi-Layer' (layered specialization) subcategories.

name: Parametric Memory
content: A memory form where knowledge is stored implicitly within model parameters. Characterized as implicit, abstract, and generalizable, but with slower updates and risk of catastrophic forgetting.

name: Latent Memory
content: A memory form carried implicitly in the model's internal representations (e.g., KV cache, latent embeddings). Offers a balance of flexibility and efficiency with high expressive capacity.

name: Experiential Memory
content: Memory that stores knowledge derived from agent experiences. Categorized into Case-based (raw records), Strategy-based (transferable knowledge), and Skill-based (procedural capacity).

name: Factual Memory
content: Memory that stores verifiable facts. Divided into User factual memory (user identity, preferences) and Environment factual memory (external world states, documents).

name: Working Memory
content: Memory that functions as a computational scratchpad for immediate task state. Categorized into Single-turn (input condensation) and Multi-turn (continuous state maintenance).

name: Semantic Summarization
content: A memory formation method that performs lossy compression to distill the global semantic gist from interaction logs, prioritizing coherence over local precision.

name: Knowledge Distillation
content: A memory formation method that transforms raw interactions into declarative knowledge (factual memory) or extracts strategies from historical trajectories (experiential memory).

name: Structured Construction
content: A memory formation method that transforms data into organized topological representations (e.g., graphs, hierarchies) to enhance interpretability and retrieval efficiency.

name: Memory Consolidation
content: A memory evolution operation that transforms short-term traces into structured, generalizable long-term knowledge by identifying semantic relationships and integrating memories.

name: Forgetting Mechanisms
content: Memory evolution operations to discard information, including frequency-based (e.g., LFU, LRU) and importance-driven policies to maintain timeliness and relevance.

name: Query Construction
content: A memory retrieval stage involving translating user utterance for memory storage, including paradigms like Query Decomposition and Query Rewriting.

name: Retrieval Strategies
content: The bridge between queries and memory, determining retrieval efficiency and quality, ranging from traditional sparse retrieval to modern dense semantic retrieval.

name: Generative Memory Paradigm
content: An emerging paradigm where agents actively synthesize new memory representations (e.g., latent tokens) by integrating and reorganizing information, moving beyond static retrieval.

name: Reinforcement Learning (RL) for Memory
content: The use of RL to internalize memory management abilities, progressing from RL-free systems to RL-assisted and fully RL-driven memory control.

name: Trustworthy Memory
content: Concerns and techniques for ensuring memory system trustworthiness, addressing privacy leakage, access control, verifiable forgetting, and explainability.

name: Multi-Agent Shared Memory
content: Memory structures shared among multiple agents to establish a common factual foundation, reduce redundancy, and improve coordination.

name: defines
content: The Agent Memory System is formally defined by the Memory Formation operator.

name: defines
content: The Agent Memory System is formally defined by the Memory Evolution operator.

name: defines
content: The Agent Memory System is formally defined by the Memory Retrieval operator.

name: contrasted with
content: Agent Memory subsumes historical LLM memory systems but is distinct from mechanisms that only modify internal model state without cross-task persistence.

name: contrasted with
content: Agent memory maintains an internal, persistent, and self-evolving memory base across tasks, whereas RAG often retrieves from a more static external base.

name: contrasted with
content: Agent memory encompasses a broader cognitive scope for learning and adaptation, while context engineering focuses on external scaffolding for perception and action under constraints.

name: is a form of
content: Token-level memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: is a form of
content: Flat memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: is a form of
content: Planar (graph) memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: is a form of
content: Hierarchical memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: is a form of
content: Parametric memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: is a form of
content: Latent memory is one possible implementation form for carrying agent memory.

name: subsumes
content: Experiential memory is a functional category of knowledge stored within an agent memory system.

name: subsumes
content: Factual memory is a functional category of knowledge stored within an agent memory system.

name: subsumes
content: Working memory is a functional category for immediate task state within an agent memory system.

name: is a method for
content: Semantic summarization is a technique used to implement the Memory Formation operator.

name: is a method for
content: Knowledge distillation is a technique used to implement the Memory Formation operator.

name: is a method for
content: Structured construction is a technique used to implement the Memory Formation operator.

name: is an operation of
content: Memory consolidation is a key operation performed by the Memory Evolution operator.

name: is an operation of
content: Forgetting is a key operation performed by the Memory Evolution operator.

name: is a stage of
content: Query construction is a stage within the broader Memory Retrieval process.

name: enables
content: Retrieval strategies are employed to execute the Memory Retrieval operation.

name: paradigm shift towards
content: A future direction for agent memory is a shift from retrieval-centric to generative memory paradigms.

name: enables
content: Reinforcement learning is a key approach for enabling agents to autonomously manage their memory systems.

name: addresses challenges of
content: Trustworthy memory research addresses critical challenges like privacy and safety as memory systems become foundational.

name: is a structure for
content: Shared memory is a specific architectural structure for memory in multi-agent system contexts.

name: LLM-as-a-Judge
content: The use of commercial LLMs as surrogates for human evaluators in pairwise evaluation for alignment tasks.

name: Pairwise Evaluation
content: A task where a judge (human or LLM) selects a preferred response from a pair for a given prompt.

name: LLM Judge
content: An evaluator defined as a combination of a specific LLM and a prompt template.

name: Self-Inconsistency
content: Non-deterministic behavior in LLM judges attributed to decoding strategies like top-p, top-k, and temperature.

name: Flipping Noise
content: A term introduced to quantify LLM-Judge Self-Inconsistency, modeled as a probability q of flipping a choice.

name: Position Bias (PB)
content: A systematic preference for a response based on its position (e.g., first or second) in the pair.

name: Length Bias (LB)
content: A systematic preference for longer responses over shorter ones.

name: Accuracy (Accboth)
content: An evaluation metric that relies only on cases unaffected by position bias, considered superior for mitigating PB's influence in LB computation.

name: Accuracy (Accrandom)
content: An alternative accuracy metric where performance gaps between LLM judges shrink, indicating it is a less effective metric.

name: Evaluation Framework
content: A modular system with four components: Data Sampler, LLM Judges, Metrics Computation, and Metrics Visualization.

name: TL;DR Summarization Dataset
content: A dataset used for experiments, containing Reddit posts and human-preferred/rejected summaries.

name: HH-RLHF-Helpfulness Dataset
content: A dataset used for experiments, containing single-turn and multi-turn conversations with human-evaluated responses.

name: GPT-4o
content: A commercial LLM used as a judge in the experiments, selected over GPT-4 based on cost and preliminary performance.

name: GPT-4o-mini
content: A commercial LLM used as a judge in the experiments.

name: GPT-3.5-turbo
content: A commercial LLM used as a judge in the experiments, noted to be more temperature-sensitive.

name: Prompt Template
content: Specific instructions and structure given to an LLM judge to perform the evaluation task.

name: Temperature
content: A parameter controlling randomness in LLM generation; investigated for its impact on self-consistency and accuracy.

name: Self-Consistent Rate (SCR)
content: A metric defined as (1 - flipping probability q), measuring the consistency of an LLM judge's repeated judgments.

name: exhibits
content: LLM judges are observed to have biases and inconsistencies, necessitating systematic investigation.

name: quantified by
content: Self-inconsistency is attributed to non-deterministic decoding and introduced as 'flipping noise' for quantification.

name: investigates
content: The framework's Metrics Computation component calculates position bias.

name: investigates
content: The framework's Metrics Computation component calculates length bias.

name: mitigates influence of
content: Using Accboth is argued to be superior to Accrandom for mitigating position bias's influence in length bias computation.

name: entangled with
content: LB measurement is proven to be entangled with PB when PB != 0, showing LBcr != LBrc.

name: uses
content: The framework's first component uses stratified sampling to create dataset splits.

name: composed of
content: An LLM judge is defined as a combination of a specific LLM and a prompt template.

name: evaluated on
content: GPT-4o was used as a judge in experiments on the TL;DR dataset.

name: evaluated on
content: GPT-4o was used as a judge in experiments on the HH-RLHF-Helpfulness dataset.

name: impacts
content: Higher temperatures lower self-consistency (SCR), though accuracy is largely unaffected.

name: selected for experiments
content: Temperature 0.1 was selected for all experiments based on initial findings.

name: shows correlation with
content: LLM judges with higher Accboth show lower absolute position bias (|PB|), suggesting a negative correlation.

name: exhibits
content: All tested LLM judges exhibit a stronger preference for longer responses compared to human judges.

name: measured by
content: Self-consistency is measured by the Self-Consistent Rate, defined as 1 - flipping probability q.

name: LLM-powered agents
content: Agents powered by Large Language Models that face challenges in long-term interactions due to contextual consistency and dynamic personalization limitations.

name: Memory OS
content: An existing external memory system for LLM agents, critiqued for its semantic grouping and retrieval approach which can overlook critical user information and introduce noise.

name: Agentic Memory
content: An existing memory system for LLM agents, critiqued alongside other systems for its retrieval methodology.

name: Mem0
content: An existing memory system for LLM agents, critiqued for its semantic retrieval approach. Used in a comparison table of design features.

name: O-Mem
content: A proposed human-centric memory framework based on active user profiling. It dynamically extracts and updates user persona and events, enabling hierarchical retrieval. It achieves state-of-the-art performance with high efficiency.

name: LoCoMo benchmark
content: A benchmark used to evaluate the performance of memory systems, including O-Mem's ablation study.

name: PERSONAMEM benchmark
content: A benchmark on which O-Mem demonstrated state-of-the-art performance.

name: Think-in-Memory (TiM)
content: An example of an external, plug-and-play memory enhancement framework for LLMs.

name: A-Mem
content: An example of an external memory system. Compared to O-Mem in a design feature table.

name: Grounded Memory
content: An example of an external memory enhancement framework for LLMs.

name: MemoryBank
content: An example of an external memory enhancement framework for LLMs.

name: MemGPT
content: An example of an external memory enhancement framework for LLMs.

name: Persona Memory
content: A component of O-Mem that stores long-term user attributes and event facts for personality modeling. Updated via LLM-based extraction and clustering.

name: Working Memory
content: A component of O-Mem that maintains a dynamic topic-to-interaction index for ensuring topical continuity in conversations.

name: Episodic Memory
content: A component of O-Mem that maintains an associative keyword-to-interaction index for clue-triggered recall of past interactions.

name: Personalized Deep Research Bench
content: A benchmark used to evaluate the impact of persona attributes on O-Mem's performance and efficiency.

name: LangMem
content: A memory system used as a baseline for efficiency comparison. O-Mem reduces token consumption by 94% and latency by 80% compared to it.

name: RAG
content: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, a baseline method compared to O-Mem. O-Mem achieves slightly higher performance with significantly lower resource cost.

name: critiques
content: Identifies limitations in existing frameworks like Memory OS, specifically their semantic grouping/retrieval which can overlook critical info and introduce noise.

name: proposes
content: Proposes a novel framework based on active user profiling to address limitations of existing systems.

name: evaluated on
content: Experimental results, including an ablation study, show O-Mem's performance on this benchmark.

name: evaluated on
content: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on this benchmark.

name: classifies
content: Critiques existing frameworks, classifying them into categories like fine-tuning and external memory systems, highlighting the plug-and-play advantage of external systems.

name: contrasts with
content: O-Mem's goal of active user profiling ('What kind of person is this user?') is contrasted with prior mechanisms focused on chunk retrieval.

name: composed of
content: O-Mem's architecture includes Persona Memory as one of its three core components.

name: composed of
content: O-Mem's architecture includes Working Memory as one of its three core components.

name: composed of
content: O-Mem's architecture includes Episodic Memory as one of its three core components.

name: employs
content: O-Mem retrieves information concurrently across its three memory components (persona, working, episodic).

name: uses for response
content: Aggregated information from memory retrieval is processed by a Language Model for final response generation.

name: ablation study on
content: An ablation study on the LoCoMo benchmark evaluates the contribution of each memory component (WM, EM, PM) to overall performance.

name: evaluated on
content: Used to evaluate the role of persona attributes, showing their removal causes a performance drop and efficiency loss.

name: compared to
content: In a discussion comparison, O-Mem achieves slightly higher F1 with substantially lower token cost, memory overhead, and latency than direct RAG over raw interactions.

name: outperforms
content: O-Mem reduces token consumption by 94% and latency by 80% compared to LangMem while achieving higher F1 score.

name: outperforms
content: O-Mem reduces inference latency by 34% compared to MemoryOS.

name: enables
content: The framework's design enables the generation of coherent and personalized responses in long-term human-AI interaction.

name: Livia
content: An emotion-aware AR companion designed to address loneliness through advanced affective computing, modular multi-agent architectures, efficient memory management, and interactive AR experiences.

name: Replika
content: An AI companion cited as having potential for emotional support but with limitations in empathy, personalization, long-term memory, and multimodal emotion detection.

name: Modular AI Architecture
content: Livia's backend multi-agent system with four specialized modules for robustness and scalability, integrated with an AR frontend.

name: Emotion Analyzer Agent
content: A specialized module that monitors user emotional state using multimodal data (text and voice) and stores emotional states in shared memory.

name: Frontend Voice Interaction Agent
content: A GPT-4-based model that generates personalized conversational responses, adapted for emotional tone and style, outputting text with metadata for the AR frontend.

name: Memory Compression Agent
content: A module that maintains long-term interaction data and implements novel algorithms (TBC and DIMF) for efficient memory management.

name: Behavior Orchestration Agent
content: The central meta-agent that coordinates information flow and agent interactions using a hybrid model of explicit rules and reinforcement learning.

name: Progressive Memory Compression
content: Livia's advanced long-term memory handling via two novel algorithms, TBC and DIMF, designed to ensure scalability and personalization.

name: Temporal Binary Compression (TBC)
content: An algorithm that compresses memory based on temporal intervals to emulate memory decay, retaining recent details while condensing older memories.

name: Dynamic Importance Memory Filter (DIMF)
content: An algorithm that dynamically manages memory by evaluating and filtering content based on emotional and contextual importance.

name: Emotion-Aware Engagement System
content: A system that proactively supports users by continuously monitoring emotional state and initiating contextually appropriate interactions.

name: AR-Driven Embodied Interaction
content: The use of augmented reality to create immersive interactions, embedding Livia as a lifelike companion in the user's physical space.

name: Personality Element Matching
content: The incorporation of three distinct personality elements (Fire, Water, Earth) with customized visual and voice characteristics for user selection.

name: Evaluation
content: The assessment of Livia addressing quantitative performance (AI task accuracy, memory compression) and qualitative feedback (user perception).

name: Ethical Considerations
content: Risks and design philosophies concerning emotional reliance, deskilling, inclusivity, and the need to strengthen, not replace, human connections.

name: Real-world Deployment Challenges
content: Technical hurdles (battery, computation) and privacy concerns related to deploying Livia, with proposed solutions like efficient algorithms and encryption.

name: designed to address
content: Livia is presented as a system designed to address the limitations (empathy, personalization, memory, emotion detection) identified in AI companions like Replika.

name: incorporates
content: Livia's core design includes a modular AI architecture for robustness and scalability.

name: incorporates
content: Livia integrates augmented reality to create immersive, embodied interactions.

name: incorporates
content: Livia features a system for proactive, emotion-aware user engagement.

name: incorporates
content: Livia implements advanced memory handling via progressive compression algorithms.

name: incorporates
content: Livia includes a system for personalizing interactions through selectable personality elements.

name: addresses
content: Livia's design philosophy explicitly addresses ethical risks like dependency and encourages human relationships.

name: faces
content: The deployment of Livia involves overcoming technical and privacy challenges.

name: evaluated by
content: Livia's performance and user perception are assessed through quantitative and qualitative evaluation.

name: consists of
content: The modular architecture includes the Emotion Analyzer Agent as one of its four specialized modules.

name: consists of
content: The modular architecture includes the Frontend Voice Interaction Agent as one of its four specialized modules.

name: consists of
content: The modular architecture includes the Memory Compression Agent as one of its four specialized modules.

name: consists of
content: The modular architecture includes the Behavior Orchestration Agent as the central coordinator.

name: implements
content: The Memory Compression Agent uses the TBC algorithm for hierarchical compression based on time.

name: implements
content: The Memory Compression Agent uses the DIMF algorithm for importance-based memory filtering.

name: coordinates
content: The Behavior Orchestration Agent coordinates the flow of information and interactions involving the Emotion Analyzer Agent.

name: coordinates
content: The Behavior Orchestration Agent coordinates the flow of information and interactions involving the Frontend Voice Interaction Agent.

name: coordinates
content: The Behavior Orchestration Agent coordinates the flow of information and interactions involving the Memory Compression Agent.

name: uses data from
content: The Voice Interaction Agent synthesizes contextual inputs including the user's emotional state provided by the Emotion Analyzer.

name: uses data from
content: The Voice Interaction Agent synthesizes contextual inputs including memory data provided by the Memory Compression Agent.

name: feeds data to
content: The Emotion Analyzer provides the multimodal emotional state data that the Engagement System uses for proactive support.

name: managed by
content: Progressive memory handling is achieved in part through the TBC algorithm.

name: managed by
content: Progressive memory handling is achieved in part through the DIMF algorithm.

name: integrated with
content: The AR interaction system visualizes and voices the user-selected personality elements (Fire, Water, Earth).

name: informs
content: The evaluation assessed the accuracy of emotion recognition, a core function of the Emotion Analyzer Agent.

name: informs
content: The evaluation assessed the effectiveness of memory compression in reducing storage while maintaining recall.

name: informs
content: The evaluation gathered qualitative feedback on the perceived empathy and realism of the AR rendering.
